title: Problem 1.1
statement_text: Let $\sigma$ and $\pi$ be two permutations of length $n$ chosen independently and uniformly at random. What is the expected value of $U(\sigma, \pi)$?
context_text: Dudek, Grytczuk and Ruciński posed the problem of determining the minimum $\ell$ for which two independently and uniformly random permutations of length $n$ are $\ell$-similar, where $U(\sigma,\pi)$ denotes the smallest such $\ell$. A simple $O(\sqrt{n})$ upper bound follows by decomposing each permutation into decreasing subpermutations via Dilworth's theorem applied to the longest increasing subsequence, which has length asymptotically $2\sqrt{n}$.
paper_contribution: Proves that two independent uniformly random permutations of length $n$ are $O\!\left(n^{1/3}\log^{11/6}(n)\right)$-similar with probability at least $1 - o(n^{-1})$, giving $E[U(\sigma,\pi)] = O\!\left(n^{1/3}\log^{11/6}(n)\right)$; this bound is tight up to a polylogarithmic factor. The result also generalises to simultaneous decompositions of $k \geq 2$ permutations.
paper_title: Decomposing random permutations into order-isomorphic subpermutations
